zh.lifeSummary: 當Jane Reyns於1329年出生在英國巴金哈姆爾克里夫頓 雷恩斯時，她的父親Sir John Reynes30歲，母親Dorothy Hastings30歲。她於1350年與Sir Richard Chamberlain II在英國巴金哈姆爾克里夫頓 雷恩斯結婚。他們至少是7個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1409年9月20日在英國貝德福爾提爾斯沃斯享年80歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Jane Reyns于1329年出生在英国巴金哈姆爾克里夫頓 雷恩斯时，她的父亲Sir John Reynes30岁，母亲Dorothy Hastings30岁。她于1350年与Sir Richard Chamberlain II在英国巴金哈姆爾克里夫頓 雷恩斯结婚。他们至少是7个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1409年9月20日在英国貝德福爾提爾斯沃斯享年80岁。

nameMeanings.name: Reyns
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Hispanic (mainly Philippines): altered form of Galician   Reino  .
Variant of   Reno  , a surname of French origin.

variants.name: Reino
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Antonio, Carlo, Carmela, Enrico, Matteo.  Spanish Jose, Juan, Luis, Pilar.
Italian: habitational name from Reino in Benevento province (Campania).
Galician: habitational name from Reino, in A Coruña and Ourense provinces.

nameMeanings.name: Jane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: Elaborated spelling of   Jane  , a form popularized no doubt by the American actress  Jayne Mansfield  (  Vera Jayne Palmer ,  1933–67 ) and British ice-dancer  Jayne Torvill  (  b. 1957 ).

variants.name: Jain
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Jane Reyns was born in 1329, in Clifton Reynes, Buckinghamshire, England, her father, Sir John Reynes, was 30 and her mother, Dorothy Hastings, was 30. She married Sir Richard Chamberlain II in 1350, in Clifton Reynes, Buckinghamshire, England. They were the parents of at least 7 sons and 1 daughter. She died on 20 September 1409, in Tilsworth, Bedfordshire, England, at the age of 80.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Jane Reyns est née en 1329, à Clifton Reynes, Buckinghamshire, Angleterre, son père, Sir John Reynes, avait 30 ans et sa mère, Dorothy Hastings, avait 30 ans. Elle a épousé Sir Richard Chamberlain II en 1350, à Clifton Reynes, Buckinghamshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 7 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée le 20 septembre 1409, à Tilsworth, Bedfordshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 80 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Jane Reyns 1329 in Clifton Reynes, Buckinghamshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir John Reynes 30 und ihre Mutter Dorothy Hastings 30. Sie hat 1350 in Clifton Reynes, Buckinghamshire, England Sir Richard Chamberlain II geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 7 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb am 20. September 1409 im Alter von 80 Jahren in Tilsworth, Bedfordshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Jane Reyns è nata il nel 1329 nel Clifton Reynes, Buckinghamshire, England, sua padre, Sir John Reynes, aveva 30 anni e sua madre, Dorothy Hastings, aveva 30 anni. Lei sposato Sir Richard Chamberlain II nel 1350, nel Clifton Reynes, Buckinghamshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 7 figli e 1 figlia. Morì il 20 settembre 1409, nel Tilsworth, Bedfordshire, England, all'età di 80 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Jane Reynsが1329年にイギリスブクインガムシュアイアクリフトン レインスで生まれた時，父Sir John Reynesは30歳，母Dorothy Hastingsは30歳であった。1350年にイギリスブクインガムシュアイアクリフトン レインスでSir Richard Chamberlain IIと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも7人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1409年9月20日にイギリスベドフォードシュアイアティルスウオルスで80歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Jane Reyns(이)가 1329년 영국 벅잉함실르 클리프턴 리인스에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir John Reynes(은)는 30세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Dorothy Hastings(은)는 30세였다. 그녀는 1350년 영국 벅잉함실르 클리프턴 리인스에서 Sir Richard Chamberlain II(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 일곱 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1409년 9월 20일 영국 베드퍼드실르 틸스워스에서 80세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Jane Reyns nasceu em 1329, em Clifton Reynes, Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir John Reynes, tinha 30 anos e sua mãe, Dorothy Hastings, tinha 30 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir Richard Chamberlain II em 1350, em Clifton Reynes, Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 7 filhos e 1 filha. Ela faleceu em 20 de setembro de 1409, em Tilsworth, Bedfordshire, Inglaterra, com 80 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Jane Reyns родилась в 1329 г. в местности клифтон ринесь, буккингхамшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir John Reynes было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Dorothy Hastings, 30. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Richard Chamberlain II в 1350 г. в местности клифтон ринесь, буккингхамшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 7 сыновей и 1 дочь. Она умерла 20 сентября 1409 г. в местности тилсворт, бедфордшир, Англия в возрасте 80 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Jane Reyns nació en 1329, en Clifton Reynes, Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir John Reynes, tenía 30 años y su mamá, Dorothy Hastings, tenía 30 años. Se casó con Sir Richard Chamberlain II en 1350, en Clifton Reynes, Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 7 hijos y 1 hija. Murió el 20 de septiembre de 1409, en Tilsworth, Bedfordshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 80 años.
